docs.title: Abstract
docs.text:
Crowd-Vision is a web app simplifying the management of a structure through a digital twin, by monitoring (and showing) in real time the people inside it, thanks to several sensors.

The goal is to allow a better management and security of movements and study through an interactive map and tools.

docs.title: Main features
docs.text:
• Real time visualization : thanks to websocket technology and sensors
  • Push server-initiated notification : security issues or overcrowded areas
  • History and data logs : sensor history and the possibility to plot them
  • LLM integration : useful for both simple queries or dashboard reports

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text: Crowd Vision is a scalable, digital-twin solution designed to monitor and visualize crowd density within building structures in real-time. The system employs an event-driven architecture to handle high-frequency data streams from IoT sensors, visualizing them on a 3D web dashboard.

docs.title: 1. High-level architecture
docs.text: The system follows a Client-Server-Database pattern, enhanced with a dedicated IoT Simulation layer. It operates on a hybrid communication model using REST APIs for configuration and WebSockets for real-time telemetry.

docs.title: Backend
docs.text:
• Runtime: Node.js with TypeScript.
  • Framework: Express.js.
  • API Documentation: OpenAPI 3.0 / Swagger UI.
  • Authentication: Custom implementation using bcryptjs for hashing.

docs.title: Frontend
docs.text:
• Framework: Vue 3.
  • Visualization: Three.js for 3D rendering of the building/digital twin.
  • Styling: Tailwind CSS.
  • Build Tool: Vite.

docs.title: Database
docs.text:
• Engine: MongoDB (v4+ implied by mongo:latest).
  • ODM: Mongoose.
  • Management: Mongo-Express for web-based database administration.

docs.title: Server Application
docs.text:
The Express application acts as the central controller.

  • REST Endpoints: handles user authentication and building configuration.

docs.title: Client Dashboard
docs.text:
An interface that allows admins and users to view status updates.

  • Views: both table view and 3d digital twin, allowing a great management.

docs.title: 4. Infrastructure & Deployment
docs.text: The application is containerized using Docker Compose, orchestrating four distinct services

docs.title: Data model
docs.text:
How are data modeled in our application? Piece of cake! To upload a building you just need a json file which describe it!

{
  "id": string,
  "rooms": [
    {
      "id": string,
      "capacity": number,
      "temperature": number,
      "no_person": number,
      "color": string,
      "position": {
        "x": number,
        "y": number,
        "z": number
      },
      "dimensions": {
        "width": number,
        "height": number,
        "depth": number
      }
    }
  ],
  "domains": [
    string
  ]
}

Thus the input simplicity, is not always easy find the correct position and dimension, allowing a great user experience!

docs.title: Sensors simulation
docs.text:
This project implements a scalable architecture for simulating an IoT sensor network.

The solution utilizes Node.js scripts integrated with Faker.js to generate stochastic, realistic environmental data. Data transmission is handled via WebSockets (Socket.io), replacing traditional HTTP polling with an event-driven "push" mechanism. The architecture employs a many-to-one multiplexing pattern, where multiple autonomous simulated clients—representing distinct classrooms—transmit data asynchronously to a centralized Express.js backend through a shared socket channel.

Identification of individual sensors is managed via unique IDs embedded within the JSON payload.

docs.title: Data exchange
docs.text:
Between the Vue.js client and the Express server there would be open a websocket where processed data from the server are pushed towards the client.

How to model data sent on the socket ???

Here the Express server should have a map containing each processor where is physically located, cause the sensor should send only its ID (if I change the sensor's position it is stupid change the sensor's configuration) ????

docs.title: Real time visualization
docs.text: Real time structure twin, allowing both generic users and admin ones to consult the status of the structure. By simulating sensors we can simulate temperature ones and movements ones too, thanks to websocket we can create a quick communication between the express server and the vue client

docs.title: Push server-initiated notifications
docs.text: Some kind of notifications, like when a certain area temperature raise above the comfort one, or when is overcrowded

docs.title: History and data logs
docs.text: An aggregate view of all data with some statistics (maybe by hours or kinda), with graphs and comparison between different structures or floors

docs.title: LLM integration
docs.text:
Campus conversational assistant: users can ask questions in natural language such as "where can I currently find a less crowded classroom to study?" or "when is the best time to go to the canteen?", and the LLM translates the request into a query on the digital twin and returns an understandable textual answer.

Automatic report generation for the admin: starting from crowd statistics, the LLM synthesizes brief reports ("this week, peak attendance shifted from library A to library B in the evening hours").

"Intelligent" push notifications: instead of static messages, the backend calls the LLM to generate different natural texts depending on the user type and the event ("area X is very crowded, we recommend Y as an alternative").
